folio.label: 55r
folio.l: ¢ Hic finitå ¸logus legis ¹iti , & incipit fabula euisdem ;

note.notetxt: 1Incipit and text same light brown ink.ODR

folio.l: Ohateful harme , condicione of pouerte
With thrust w cold , with honger so confounded
To asken helpe , the shametú in thyn herte
If thow noon aske , so sore art æow I wounded
That verray nede , vnwrappeth alle thy wounde hid
Maugre thyn heede , thow must for indigence
Or stele or begge , or borwe thyn dispence
¢ Thow blamest crist , and seist ful bitterly
He mysdepartetú , ricchesse temporal
Thy neyghebore , thow wytest synfully
And seist æè hast to lite , and he hatú al
Parfey seist æè , sumtyme he rekne shal
Whan æ his taylle , shal brennen in the glede
For he nougút helpetú , nedeful in here nede
¢ Herkeneth what is the sentence , of the wyse
Bette is to deyen , than haue indigence
Thy selue neyghebore , wyl the despise
If thow be pore , fare wel thy reuerence
ñet if the wyse man , take this sentence
Alle the dayes of poore men , ben wykke
Be war æäfore , er æè come to æ prikke
¢ If æè be pore , thy brother hatetú the
And alle thyne frendes , flen from the , allas
O riche marchauntez , ful of wele ben ©e
O noble o prudent folk , as in this cas
ñoure bagges be nou©t filled , w aumbes as
But with sys synk , æ rennetú for ©oure chaunce
